items.back: 1. Aircraft approx 15 miles from destination AND you no longer desire transponder/ADS-B operation. 2. Necessary to reduce clutter in multi-target area — MUST instruct pilot to return to normal as soon as possible thereafter.

items.front: 5-2-10: Key difference between the two standby conditions
items.back: Clutter-reduction standby requires instructing the pilot to return to normal as soon as possible. The 15-mile destination standby carries no such return-instruction obligation.

items.front: 5-2-12: Two transponder failure scenarios and their phraseology
items.back: 1. Code not displaying (operable transponder): 'RESET TRANSPONDER, SQUAWK (code).' 2. Appears inoperative: 'YOUR TRANSPONDER APPEARS INOPERATIVE/MALFUNCTIONING, RESET, SQUAWK (code).' Both require notifying subsequent control position.

items.front: 5-2-14: Basis for disapproving or withdrawing approval for failed transponder/ADS-B Out
items.back: May disapprove or withdraw approval SOLELY on the basis of traffic conditions or other operational factors.

items.front: 5-2-15: Four triggers for Mode C altitude validation
items.back: 1. Initial track start. 2. Track start from coast/frozen. 3. During/after unreliable Mode C readout. 4. Accepting interfacility handoff (with exceptions for CTRD tower cabs and ERAM-to-ERAM).

items.front: 5-2-15: Three ways a Mode C readout is considered valid
items.back: 1. Varies less than 300 ft from pilot-reported altitude. 2. Continuous readout from aircraft on airport varies < 300 ft from field elevation. 3. Correlated with validated data block from another facility and readouts are exactly the same.

items.front: 5-2-15: Procedure for invalid Mode C below FL180 (after altimeter check fails)
items.back: 1. Instruct pilot to STOP ALTITUDE SQUAWK, state reason (ALTITUDE DIFFERS BY (number) FEET). 2. Notify operations supervisor-in-charge of aircraft call sign.

items.front: 5-2-15: Procedure for invalid Mode C at/above FL180 (after 29.92 check fails)
items.back: Same as below FL180: STOP ALTITUDE SQUAWK with reason, and notify operations supervisor-in-charge. Additionally verify pilot uses 29.92 and has accurately reported altitude.

items.front: 5-2-16: Four exceptions to Mode C altitude confirmation on initial contact
items.back: 1. Pilot states assigned altitude. 2. New altitude assigned to climbing/descending aircraft. 3. Valid Mode C readout shows aircraft established at assigned altitude. 4. TERMINAL: intrafacility transfer from another sector/position.

items.front: 5-2-16 vs 5-2-17: Difference in altitude confirmation exceptions
items.back: Mode C (5-2-16) has the extra exception: valid Mode C readout shows aircraft is established at assigned altitude. Non-Mode C (5-2-17) lacks this since there is no Mode C to read.

items.front: 5-2-17b (USA): Controller response to pilot altitude readback
items.back: MUST reconfirm ALL pilot altitude readbacks. Correct: 'AFFIRMATIVE (altitude).' Incorrect: 'NEGATIVE. CLIMB/DESCEND AND MAINTAIN (altitude)' or 'NEGATIVE. MAINTAIN (altitude).'

items.front: 5-2-19: Scope — altitude and airspace for inflight Mode C deviation procedures
items.back: 48 contiguous states and DC, at and above 10,000 ft MSL and below 18,000 ft MSL, excluding airspace at and below 2,500 ft AGL.

items.front: 5-2-19: Three-step sequence for VFR deviation request (inoperative transponder/Mode C)
items.back: 1. Suggest airspace unaffected by CFRs. 2. Suggest IFR flight plan. 3. Suggest VFR route with radio contact. Do NOT approve unless IFR filed or VFR route provided with radio contact.

items.front: 5-2-22: Three items to report to OS/CIC for inoperative ADS-B transmitter
items.back: Aircraft call sign, location, and time of occurrence (UTC).

items.front: 5-2-22: Which aircraft are excepted from pilot notification of inoperative ADS-B?
items.back: DoD aircraft and those provided for in 5-2-24 (ADS-B Out OFF authorized operations).

items.front: 5-2-22: When must the controller instruct an aircraft to stop ADS-B transmissions?
items.back: When the malfunctioning ADS-B transmitter is jeopardizing the safe execution of ATC functions. Phraseology: 'STOP ADS-B TRANSMISSIONS, AND IF ABLE, SQUAWK THREE/ALFA (code).'

items.front: 5-2-23: Two types of ADS-B alerts and their meanings
items.back: 1. CSMM (Call Sign Mis-Match): transmitted FLT ID does not match flight plan aircraft ID. 2. Duplicate ICAO Address: broadcast ICAO address shared with another flight in same ADS-B Service Area — target resolution may be lost.

items.front: 5-2-24: Controller actions for ADS-B Out OFF operations
items.back: 1. Do NOT inform such aircraft their ADS-B transmitter appears inoperative. 2. Do NOT approve any pilot request for ADS-B Out OFF — notify OS/CIC with call sign and location. Authorization comes only from FAA System Operations Security.
